summary: Stellar first set and epic Playing In The Band with standout Bird Song
blurb: The show opens strong with New Minglewood Blues, Sugaree and the Lazy Lightning > Supplication pair, plus It Must Have Been The Roses. The second set kicks off with a rare Bird Song, then rolls into an expansive Playing In The Band, Terrapin Station and The Other One. Overall the band is tight and Brent’s organ shines.
review: March 23, 1981 at London’s Rainbow Theatre captures one of the Dead’s most cohesive early‑’80s outings. Jerry’s guitar soars on New Minglewood Blues and throughout a first set that fans still cite as a fan favorite, while Brent’s Hammond organ adds a warm, full texture that lifts the whole performance. The second half delivers a rare Bird Song opening, leading into a seamless segue through Samson & Delilah, To Lay Me Down, Terrapin Station and an expansive Playing In The Band that showcases the band’s tight interplay and dynamic drumming from Mickey and Billy. Highlights like The Other One, Stella Blue and Sugar Magnolia close out a show that feels both adventurous and tightly locked‑in, making it a must‑listen for anyone exploring this era.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Both recordings are described with positive sentiment, praising the setlist flow, standout songs (Bird Song, Playing In The Band, New Minglewood Blues) and overall band cohesion. No negative comments appear across analyses, indicating strong consensus that the performance is exceptional.

best_recording.reason: Warm, up‑close audience tape that captures the full show with clear instrument separation; despite a slight tinny high end it delivers the most complete and enjoyable listening experience of the available sources.

key_highlights: First set praised as a fan favorite with strong song placement
Rare Bird Song opening the second set followed by an epic Playing In The Band
Brent’s Hammond organ shines on New Minglewood Blues and throughout the jam sections
song_highlights: It Must Have Been The Roses

band_performance.Jerry: Spectacular solos on New Minglewood Blues and consistently crisp, soaring leads throughout the set.
band_performance.Phil: Bass is less audible in the audience mixes, described as a bit thin, but maintains solid foundation.
band_performance.Brent: Fantastic Hammond organ work, especially on New Minglewood Blues, adding depth to the jams.

must_listen_sequences: It Must Have Been The Roses
